blocks.content: Peope's face froze upon hearing Shirone's words.
She couldn't comprehend the phenomenon unfolding within her heart.
As hope for survival emerged, the intense emotions that had dominated her began to vanish like bubbles.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Are you mocking me? What do you gain by humiliating me?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mocking? Don't talk nonsense. Saving someone who begs for their life is the natural thing to do. Because I want to live too. Since my life is precious, I understand how precious others' lives are.
Shirone hadn't decided to let Peope go. What judgment he would make at the final moment remained uncertain.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But he thought of Kanya and Rena. He couldn't let them die like this.
I'll count to three. One.
His grip on Peope tightened.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: P-please spare me.
As if he hadn't heard, Shirone didn't relax his stance. Just as he was about to say 'three,' Peope, who had tightly shut her eyes, screamed.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Spare me! I'm begging you! I don't want to die!
Shirone released Peope.
Freed, she fluttered her wings and flew up. Her face flushed like a sunset as she glared down at him.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Do you think you can get away with this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What did I even do?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: You tried to kill me!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I did. And that's exactly what you did to Kanya and Rena.
Peope couldn't refute it.
Born as an enforcer of the law, she had never deeply contemplated the lifespans of the people.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: But now that her life was in someone else's hands, it was a complicated matter.
Death was terrifying, and life was sweet. Even for a fairy who lived for ten thousand years.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I also think what happened to Kanya's mother was wrong. Restore her lifespan too.
Before she could even gather her thoughts, the pressure made Peope snap.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: I already told you! Only the upper echelons of the 72 fairy ranks can tamper with the Book of Lifespans. A settled judgment can't be overturned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then at least rejudge Kanya and Rena's case. You can do that, right?
Peope, lost in thought with her chin propped, spoke.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Well… fine, I'll reduce their sentence to one year.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone let out a hollow laugh.
While it was good that Kanya and Rena's punishment was lightened, the gap in judgment was too vast.
It seemed there had never been any standards to begin with.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are you some junk dealer? How can you pass judgments so arbitrarily?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: This is already me being generous! Should I raise it to 20 years instead?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's not the point. Doesn't this seem wrong to you? There should be standards for applying punishment.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: That's why I'm the standard. I'm the enforcer of the law! Are you really this ignorant?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone bit his tongue. He had no choice but to acknowledge that fairies were a different species from humans, diverging at some point.
Fairy thought processes were more holistic than human ones.
While humans could dissect love into countless concepts, to fairies, love was just love.
Peope was the enforcer of the law, and thus she never doubted her own judgments.
Knowing how terrifying death was, she reduced the sentence to one year.
It was understandable that she couldn't make detailed judgments like '2 years and 3 months' or '7 years and 6 months.'
After thousands of years, her thinking might deepen, but currently, she was barely a year old.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are we done here? Then I'll be going. Fairies have a lot of work to do.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Going where? You still need to resolve the issue with Kanya's mother, don't you?
Peope turned to Shirone with a glare.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: How am I supposed to resolve that? I wasn't even born when it happened. Besides, my rank is the lowest among the 72 fairy ranks—I don't have the authority to modify the Book of Lifespans.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then at least tell me how. You're a fairy, so you must know something, right? I won't let you go until you tell me.
Peope, true to her nature as a spiral spirit, was stubborn, but perhaps due to her youth, she wasn't too difficult to handle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he could extract information from her, he might find a way to save Kanya's mother.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Hmph, do you think you can stop me from leaving? And no one can interfere with fairy affairs. That's the law.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know what that law is, but even you know it's unreasonable, right? Life is precious. How can you arbitrarily cut someone's lifespan by 20 years?
Peope's eyes welled up with tears.
Truthfully, she didn't know either.
To judge the law's right or wrong by personal standards, she'd need to live at least 600 years.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Why are you always targeting me? I'm just following orders!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Weren't you the one who said you're the enforcer of the law? Where's all that confidence now? Are you running away?

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Ugh, seriously! What do you want me to do? What do you want to know?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: First, tell me what the Wine of Ilhwa is.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: The Wine of Ilhwa? It's a sacred ritual. The laws of the people are refined, and new life is reborn.
But Shirone sensed nothing but packaged righteousness in her words.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How am I supposed to understand that? Be specific. What is its purpose, how is it conducted, what are the ritual's components—things like that.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Ugh, you're so annoying…!
Peope clutched her head with both hands.
The Wine of Ilhwa was just the Wine of Ilhwa.
She had never reflected on the information she knew as innate knowledge.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: If you really want to know, there's a way to explain it properly. Through mental resonance. Fairies can share memories with others. I'll show you my memory of the Wine of Ilhwa. Will that suffice?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mental resonance was dangerous.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Peope didn't seem like she'd do anything malicious, but she wasn't an ally either.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: -It's fine. I can handle someone like Peope.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: -Are you sure? No matter how strong your defenses are, there's always the outlier factor.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: -Peope is emotionally immature, so I can detect the unique patterns of outlier techniques immediately. Then, I can knock her out with a mental shock before she activates anything.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It seemed Canis and the others had also experienced outlier techniques before.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thinking about how formidable precognition was, Shirone conveyed his consent to Peope.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. Show me your memory of the Wine of Ilhwa.

blocks.speaker: Peope
blocks.content: Alright. Here goes.
Peope cast the memory transfer spell.
As the minds of Shirone and his companions were pulled into the memory, the scene from a year ago unfolded.
It was the square where the giant statue stood, and the people were so quiet they didn't even breathe.
When Shirone called out to his friends who had arrived at different points, they approached, passing through the people like ghosts.
Though it was someone else's memory, it felt as vivid as reality.
As a memory entity, Shirone could observe anything he wanted.
It was fascinating how he could stick his face right in front of a person without them noticing.
Shirone and his group headed toward the statue.
As the crowd's barrier vanished, an astonishing sight unfolded.
Multiple hoses were connected to the giant statue, linked to eight glass orbs arranged around it.
The orbs were large enough to hold a person.
The people were silent, but some were crying.
The mix of reverence and despair in the scene was unsettling.
Kergoin scurried around the statue, preparing the ritual.
Eight people stepped forward from the crowd.
They were mostly in their 50s or 60s, but one was a young woman.
Shirone saw tears behind their smiles.
Trembling pupils, quivering lips, cold sweat on their foreheads.
They were clearly thinking of death.
The subjects entered the glass orbs, wearing nothing.
As black liquid filled the orbs, their families wailed and rushed forward.
Meanwhile, the other people prostrated themselves and began chanting 'Anke Ra.'
The families, crying out for their mothers, fathers, and grandfathers, pounded on the glass, but Kergoin pulled them away.
The subjects submerged in the rising liquid smiled.
Was this drowning?
It was cruel.
But as Shirone peered into the orbs, he recoiled in horror.
It wasn't drowning. Their bodies were dissolving in the liquid.
It was the most grotesque sight he had ever seen.
He wondered what the liquid was made of, and why it was black.
If the families had seen this process, they might have gone mad.
The liquid in the orbs was sucked through the hoses.
A disgusting sound, like intestines expelling waste, filled the air.
The people's chants of 'Ra' grew louder.
As the liquid level dropped, the orbs' interiors became transparent again, devoid of any residue.
There was nothing left.
The people who had been smiling moments ago had vanished through the hoses.
Shirone turned his gaze to where the hoses led.
His body was already trembling.
Yet, he had to see.
The giant statue.
There, at the base of the statue, the eight people poured out.
Then, what was it? What was rising inside the statue now?
The eternal Ra.
The chants of 'Anke Ra' pierced the sky.
Suddenly, the giant statue trembled violently.
At that moment, the people fell silent.
Not a drop of liquid remained in the orbs.
It had all been poured into the giant statue.
Like a mold.
Shirone shook his head.
It couldn't be.
But once the thought came, he couldn't stop it.
A mold filled with melted humans.
Then, what was being forged inside…?
The giant statue split open along a seam.
A massive foot emerged, shaking the ground.
Shirone shuddered at the sight of the giant, coated in a jelly-like substance.
Its expression was too distorted to call human.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: The reason he couldn't sense the giant's mind was that multiple consciousnesses were tangled within a single body.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: So, what state were those minds in?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Were they the eight separate minds, or something entirely different?
The people begged for mercy.
Born of the law, bound by the law. The giant.
Thus, the giant had the right to judge those who broke the law.
At this moment, even if someone were crushed under the giant's foot, it wouldn't be the giant's fault.
People who met the giant's eyes foamed at the mouth and collapsed.
It was the eye technique, Pressing.
Ah.
Shirone trembled.
The origin of Schemata.
The primordial Schemata was the phenomenon of human blueprints overlapping through the Wine of Ilhwa.
Following the giant, he eavesdropped on the people's conversations.
The giant would now leave heaven and enter purgatory.
There, it would judge the heretics threatening heaven before returning to the Fifth Heaven, Mathey, upon Ra's call.
The families cried out in tears.
Mom! Dad! Grandpa!
As if responding to the voices, the giant twitched.
But it didn't look back, continuing its march toward the gates as if nothing had happened.
The scenery of Peope's memory burned away like paper, and the house's interior returned.
Shirone and his companions looked around.
Peope was gone.
It seemed she had fled during the memory playback.
But now that they knew what the Wine of Ilhwa was, they had no time to dwell on it.
The subjects of the Wine of Ilhwa dissolved in the liquid.
Then, they were poured into a mold, underwent a special transformation process, and were reborn as giants.
The people's minds became entangled, and in that process, Schemata emerged.
That was the meaning behind the awakening drug Kanya had bought.
It was a desperate hope to retain even a sliver of consciousness in the unknown darkness.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: This can't be…
Tess's face was pale.
The giant's ability, Schemata.
And his own ability, Schemata.
When his thoughts reached that point, he ran to a corner and vomited.
The food he had just eaten came back up.
No one blamed Tess.
They all felt sick.
As Rian approached, Tess raised a hand to stop him.
Of course, Peope's memory wasn't more gruesome than a battlefield.
But the mental nausea was overwhelming.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Now you understand why I didn't tell you.
Some called it a blessing, others a curse.
For this event, debated even among the people and heretics, no one wanted to speak of it.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: According to the law, humans were born from giants. Thus, returning to giants. That is the Wine of Ilhwa.
Even if Kanya's words were true, it was impossible to accept.
The Wine of Ilhwa Shirone had witnessed was a monstrosity no euphemism could disguise.
— Twelve years later —
